text.title: Python Type Hints — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when python-type-hints is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.description: def f(x: Any) -> Any: ... in new code.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: No type checking; IDE autocomplete dies. Fix: Replace with concrete types; if truly unknown, use object.

antipattern.description: # type: ignore at end of line, no comment.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Hides real bugs; impossible to audit. Fix: Use # type: ignore[<code>] # reason: <why>.

antipattern.description: from typing import List, Optional, Union.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Adds import; deprecated alias. Fix: Use built-in generics and X | None.

antipattern.description: Pydantic model parsing inside a tight per-iteration loop.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Validation overhead dominates; CPU bound. Fix: Use dataclasses internally; Pydantic only at the boundary.

antipattern.description: Public function with no type hints.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Consumers cannot use IDE; mypy strict fails. Fix: Type every public function fully.
